evidence: Source (es_compat.rs:10175) iterates body.actions calling engine.add_alias/remove_alias on the live Arc<DashMap<String,Vec<String>>>. Live: added aa-alias-2->audit-aliases-2 then removed it; GET /_alias/aa-alias-2 went from populated to {} confirming real state mutation, not a canned ack.

Source (10190) calls engine.list_indices() and folds in every alias whose value contains the index. Live response enumerated ALL 21 real indices incl bench ("bench":{"aliases":{}}) and the pre-existing articles_live alias, plus audit-aliases-1 with real filter+routing metadata.

Source (14624) mirrors get_aliases over engine.list_indices(). Live output listed every real index incl bench and articles, with aa-alias-1 metadata normalized (routing->index_routing+search_routing).
Source (14530) returns 200 iff engine.aliases is non-empty. Live HEAD /_alias returned 200 (aliases present); reflects real map state.

Source (14650) supports comma/wildcard/_all matching and returns indices carrying the alias. Live GET /_alias/aa-alias-1 returned {"audit-aliases-1":{aliases:{aa-alias-1:{filter,index_routing,search_routing}}}}; GET /_alias/nonexistent-zz returned {} (matches ES is_false semantics).
Source (14542) returns 200 iff some real index carries a matching alias. Live: HEAD /_alias/aa-alias-1 -> 200, HEAD /_alias/nope-zz -> 404. Correct discrimination against real alias state.

Real alias data for real indices: GET /audit-aliases-1/_alias returned the alias+metadata, GET /bench/_alias returned {"bench":{aliases:{}}}. BUT GET /no-such-index-zz/_alias returned 200 {"no-such-index-zz":{aliases:{}}} instead of ES's 404 index_not_found. resolve_index_selector (10243) pushes an exact name unconditionally, so the handler's is_empty()->404 branch (14692) is dead for single names — divergence from ES error behavior.

Source (14573) returns 200 iff a resolved index actually carries an alias. Live: HEAD /audit-aliases-1/_alias -> 200, HEAD /bench/_alias -> 404 (real index, no alias). Reflects genuine per-index alias state.

Source (10251) resolves index selector (incl wildcards), calls engine.add_alias and stores filter/routing in index_alias_metadata. Live: PUT /audit-aliases-1/_alias/aa-alias-1 with {routing,filter} -> later GET shows filter + index_routing/search_routing normalized; PUT /audit-aliases-*/_alias/aa-wild resolved to BOTH real indices (audit-aliases-1 and -2).
es_compat.rs:384 sums real idx.doc_count + dir_size_bytes(data_dir). Live: 'bench ... 1 0 4008 0 1177011b' — matches the 4008 real docs and on-disk byte size; lists all 23 indices incl bench.

es_compat.rs:9663 uses engine.health().index_count for shard count + live epoch/clock. Live: '1782799805 06:10:05 xerj green 1 1 21 21 ...' shards=21 == real index count. Single-node green/1/1/100% are acceptable fixed values.

es_compat.rs:9688 reads /proc meminfo, RSS, loadavg, samples CPU. Live: '127.0.0.1 0 7 10 1.16 0.94 0.90 ... local' — load ~matches /proc/loadavg 1.08/0.92/0.89, ram 7% matches MemAvailable/MemTotal, name='local' is real engine.node_id.
es_compat.rs:12760 iterates engine.aliases. Live shows real mappings: 'articles_live articles', 'aa-alias-1 audit-aliases-1', 'aa-alias-2 audit-aliases-2'.
es_compat.rs:12786 returns idx.stats().doc_count. Live /_cat/count/bench: '... 4008' == real bench doc count.
es_compat.rs:12815 real doc_count + dir_size_bytes per index. Live: 'bench 0 p STARTED 4008 1177011b ...'. (Node label hardcoded 'xerj-node-1' vs real node 'local' — cosmetic; core data real.)
es_compat.rs:14144 iterates engine.templates. Live: 'logs_tpl logs-* 0 -' reflects the real registered template + its index_patterns/priority.
read_disk_stats() now statvfs(data_dir). Live _cat/allocation total/avail (64019849216/63220850688) matches `df -B1 /tmp` exactly; disk.indices = real sum of index data_dirs.

es_compat.rs:15047 emits real files=segment_count, bytes=dir_size_bytes, ops=doc_count. Live bench row: '... 1 1 100.0% 1 1177011 1177011 100.0% 1177011 4008 4008 100.0%' — real bytes + 4008 docs. (stage=done/existing_store synthesized, fine for single-node.)

es_compat.rs:15085: docs.count=4008 and size=1177011b are real (idx.stats + dir_size_bytes), but it collapses to one synthetic segment '_0' gen 0, size.memory hardcoded 0, and version '9.10.0' fabricated (xerj uses a memtable, not Lucene segments).

Honest 0 active/queue/rejected across the real pool set (search/write/bulk/get/.../generic); xerj has no live per-pool counters so 0 is truthful when idle (no longer fabricates a CPU-count active value).

es_compat.rs:15141 enumerates real indices, but size is hardcoded '0b' for every index (no fielddata cache) and node column hardcoded 'xerj-node-1' (inconsistent with real node id 'local'). Only the index list is real.

es_compat.rs:15165 ignores State and always builds an empty Vec -> empty body. Live returns empty even during a running reindex. Honest for single-node (no cluster-state queue) but consults no real state.
es_compat.rs:15182 returns a hardcoded empty String, takes no State. Live: empty body. Correct (xerj has no plugin system) but pure constant.
es_compat.rs:15193 returns hardcoded empty String (comment notes prior fabricated ml.* rows were removed). Live: empty. Honest but a constant that reflects no live state.
es_compat.rs:15206 uses real engine.node_id. Live: 'local 127.0.0.1 127.0.0.1 local' — id matches the real node_id surfaced by /_cat/nodes.
es_compat.rs:18623 returns hardcoded "", no State. Live: empty. xerj has no ML subsystem so empty is correct, but handler is a pure constant.
es_compat.rs:18631 returns hardcoded "", no State. Live: empty. No ML subsystem; constant placeholder.
es_compat.rs:18639 returns hardcoded "", no State. Live: empty. No ML subsystem; constant placeholder.
Now lists the real TaskRegistry. Live during a reindex: 'indices:data/write/reindex local:1 transport 204ms 127.0.0.1 local'. es_compat.rs cat_tasks iterates state.tasks.list().
es_compat.rs:19212 iterates engine.snapshot_repos. Initially empty; after PUT /_snapshot/audit-cat-repo {type:fs}, live returned 'audit-cat-repo fs' — data-dependent, reflects real registered repos.
Now uses stable state.engine.node_id (was a fresh random uuid per call). Live: name='local' identical across calls, matching _cat/nodes/_cat/master.

Reads state.engine.list_indices() and per-index index_settings. Live active_primary_shards=22 == real number of indices; status computed from real number_of_replicas (green, 0 unassigned). Genuine engine state, not a constant.

Same cluster_health_inner with real index filter. Live /_cluster/health/bench -> active_shards=1; ?level=indices returns indices.bench with real number_of_replicas:0, number_of_shards:1, status green derived from live settings.

Real signals: indices.docs.count=16043 from engine.health(), heap derived from read_rss_bytes() (/proc/self/status). Fabricated: os.cpu.percent:0 (does NOT reflect /proc/loadavg ~1.1), os.mem = rss*4/rss*3 synthetic, store.size_in_bytes = docs*256 (=4107008, not real bytes), thread_pool fixed {threads:4}, indexing/search totals hardcoded 0, transport handshake stats canned, dense_vector bytes synthesised.

Real: indices.count=23, docs.count=16043, RSS-derived heap/mem. Fabricated: store.size_in_bytes=docs*256=4107008, nodes.os.available_processors:1 (real machine = 32 per nproc), segments.count:0, replication:0.0, versions hardcoded ["8.13.0"], cluster_uuid constant "xerj-cluster-1".

Index names are real from list_indices() (live output includes bench and all .xerj_* system indices). But per-index metadata is fabricated: number_of_shards hardcoded "1", number_of_replicas "0", mappings:{} always empty, index uuid is Uuid::new_v4() regenerated every call (non-stable), routing_table all STARTED on hardcoded "xerj-node-1", master_node/cluster_uuid constants.

Uses real node_id (current_node.id="local" == engine.node_id) and real index selection: default picked first user index "logs-2026-04", body {index:bench} echoed with real existence check. But allocation decisions are canned single-node text, current_state always "started", weight_ranking hardcoded 1; no real allocator runs. Source header still labels it a stub upgraded for single-node honesty.
Reads state.engine.cluster_settings RwLock. After PUT of persistent.audit.test.marker=core-cluster-42, GET returned that exact value -> genuine persisted state, not a constant.

Writes persistent/transient into engine.cluster_settings write lock. Live PUT {persistent:{audit.test.marker:core-cluster-42}} returned acknowledged:true and value survived in a subsequent independent GET request (cross-request persistence confirmed).

Derives real values: routing_summary.index_count=22 from list_indices(), node_id="local" from engine.node_id. Honest, but it is intrinsically a no-op (single-node) and performs no shard movement; relocating/initializing hardcoded 0 with explanation that nothing is moved.

Signature State(_state) ignores engine; body builds an empty Vec and returns {"tasks":[]} unconditionally. Live returned []. Semantically valid for single-node (no master queue) but no engine state is ever consulted.

Real: os.available_processors=32 & allocated_processors=32 (==nproc), process pid=169890 (==std::process::id()), version "Rust 1.0.0-rc.1" from CARGO_PKG_VERSION, docs.count=16043. Fabricated/wrong: os.arch hardcoded "aarch64" while machine is x86_64 (uname -m), heap_*_in_bytes synthesised from RSS, store.size=docs*256, node id hardcoded "xerj-node-1", ml.* attributes synthetic.

Body: Path(_node_id) ignored, delegates verbatim to nodes_stats(). Live /_nodes/xerj-node-1/stats returns identical payload to /_nodes/stats (real docs.count=16043 plus the same fabricated cpu/mem/thread_pool/store fields). Any node id, including bogus, returns the same single node.

Iterates engine.index_settings, but that map is empty for these indices so live routing_table={} despite 23 indices and node shard_count=0. All balance numbers are sentinels: cluster_balance_stats metrics =1e-9/1.0, forecast/actual_disk_usage=0, cluster_info total_bytes=0, node hardcoded "xerj-node-1", stats.computation_* all =1. Response surfaces no genuine balance/disk state.

NDJSON 2 index actions -> items result:created status:201; GET /audit-doc-crud-idx/_doc/g1 found:true source={model:m1,cost_usd:1.5}. src:8770 delegates to process_bulk_body(None) -> bulk::process_bulk_with_opts.
POST returned 201 with server-gen _id ce74e9c9..; subsequent GET of that id found:true source={model:auto,cost_usd:9.9}. src:1398 calls idx.index_document(None,doc).await, real persist.

PUT p1 ->201 created; re-PUT ->HTTP 200, GET shows new source {model:put1b,cost_usd:4.4}. Quirk: update body still says result:created (EsDocResponse::created hardcoded) though status correctly 200. src:1515.
First PUT _create/c1 ->201; repeat ->409 version_conflict_engine_exception, GET c1 unchanged {model:cr,cost_usd:1.1}. src:1651 idx.create_document(id) enforces existence -> genuine.
doc-merge: p1 update cost_usd:99+newfield merged, model retained, result:updated; doc_as_upsert created up1; no-upsert missing999 ->404 document_missing. src:9377 update_document_with_upsert.
term model:DELME (2 docs) ->deleted:2; afterwards search DELME total:0 and GET del1/del2 found:false 404. src:12603 searches then idx.delete_document per hit.
updated:1 returned and doc reindexed, but script ctx._source.status=new NOT applied: GET uq1 status still 'old'. src:12685 comment 'no script execution', reindexes hit.source as-is.
mixed index/update/delete: b1 created(GET found), uq1 update cost_usd:777 persisted, keep1 delete ->GET 404. src:8755 process_bulk_body(Some(index)) -> real bulk pipeline.

Returns mappings for every real index (.ds-audit-lifecycle, .xerj_alert_*, bench...). Delegates get_mapping(_all); reads engine.index_mappings/schema (src 1267,1275).
Per-index settings with real uuids/creation_date for all indices. Delegates get_settings(_all) reading engine.index_settings + merge_settings_defaults (src 1271,1358).
Added {newfield:keyword} to audit-index-mgmt-idx -> acknowledged; subsequent GET _mapping shows n,name,newfield. idx.add_field + merges into stored mappings (src 1131-1148).
GET bench/_mapping returns all 12 real fields with correct types (cost_usd:double, latency_ms:long, cache_hit:boolean...). Reads engine.index_mappings (src 1275).
bench/_mapping/field/model -> {full_name:model,mapping:{model:{type:text}}}, matches schema. Reads idx.schema(); note only emits `type`, drops other field params (src 16756).
bench/_settings shows real uuid 6ebb722c, creation_date, number_of_shards:1. Reads engine.index_settings + merge_settings_defaults (src 1358).
PUT refresh_interval:30s -> acknowledged; re-GET _settings shows refresh_interval:30s merged in. Merges into engine.index_settings index block (src 12839-12863).
PUT _block/read_only -> blocked:true; subsequent PUT _doc returns 403 cluster_block_exception 'blocked for write operations'. idx.set_block persists index.blocks (src 16825; index.rs:5194).
bench docs.count:4008 real; live get{total:4,exists:3,missing:1} and request_cache.miss_count:2 from atomic counters. Note store size=docs*256 estimate & segments.count hardcoded 0 (src 10016-10100).

store_size_in_bytes=1026048 = doc_count(4008)*256 estimate, scales w/ real count; but all_fields breakdown synthesized (inverted_index/doc_values/points all 0b despite text index), fields:{}. Comment: 'synthesise minimal' (src 9889).
Returns {_shards:successful:1}; handler calls idx.flush().await doing real memtable->segment work (src 9415-9417).
'The Quick Brown Foxes' standard -> [the,quick,brown,foxes] lowercased w/ correct offsets+positions (matches ES standard analyzer). Real tokenize_for_analyze (src 9719).
'Hello World' whitespace -> [Hello,World] case preserved, offsets 0-5/6-11. Delegates analyze_text(_none) (src 9712).

Sets closed_indices flag (visible to GET-index/cat expand_wildcards) but search on closed idx2 still returns 200 hits total:1 — no index_closed_exception enforcement on _search/_doc (src 13860-13871).
Removes closed_indices flag (acknowledged) and re-evaluates @timestamp index-sort from current mapping/probe. Genuine state change via closed_indices.remove (src 13877-13935).
Returns merged_batches:0; handler loops idx.run_merge_once() (real engine merge driver) until 0 selected — genuine call, 0 because nothing mergeable (src 13942-13960).
{_shards:successful:1}; engine.flush_index drains memtable shards to durable segment + WAL checkpoint (src 14017-14024).
_shards total:23 successful:23 — iterates engine.list_indices() and flush_index each, real per-index success count (src 14044-14064).
Returns canned {_shards:{total:1,successful:1,failed:0}}; handler binds State as _state, Path as _index, no engine call at all (src 14079-14086).
bench fsck walks real segment (segment_id 8ed2bb08), recomputes CRC32C: total_segments_checked:1, total_sections_checked:1, Stored ok:true, corrupt:0. idx.fsck_segments() (src 13983-14001).
Cloned idx2->audit-index-mgmt-clone; target _count=1 matches source. clone_index_to creates target w/ src schema + copies docs via match_all (caveat: size=10000 cap) (src 15184-15229).
audit-index-mgmt-shrink _count=1 (docs copied) but body identical to clone_index_to — no real shard reduction, target always 1 shard; defining shrink semantic absent (src 15248-15254).
audit-index-mgmt-split _count=1 (docs copied) but just calls clone_index_to — no shard multiplication, defining split semantic (increasing shard count) absent (src 15265-15271).
bench stage:DONE; files.total/reused from real stats.segment_count(1) but size totals all 0, translog 0, times 0, target node hardcoded xerj-node-1:9300 — mostly canned (src 18288-18345).
bench: 1 segment, num_docs:4008, size_in_bytes:159052, generation from min_seq_no — real store_snapshot().segments. version/memory cosmetic (src 18356-18402).
Inserts engine.frozen_indices flag (genuine state) but freeze has no enforced functional effect / not surfaced; pure ack otherwise (src 18417-18428).
Removes engine.frozen_indices flag; toggles same unenforced state as freeze, returns ack (src 18434-18445).
After PUT, GET returned {"audit-ingest-enrich-pipe":{...processors...}}; empty {} before. Source: iterates state.engine.pipelines map (L12994). Reflects live state.
Inline set+uppercase actually applied: name alice->ALICE, env=prod added, keep retained. Source L13003 validates+runs run_inline_pipeline_on_doc. Genuine transform.
{"acknowledged":true}; inserts into engine.pipelines + create_pipeline (L12883/12937). Verified: GET-all and :id/_simulate both reflect it afterward. Persists.
Stored pipe executed: old:v renamed->new:v, env=prod set. Source L13800 looks up stored pipeline, 404 if missing, runs processors. Real exec.
{"acknowledged":true}; inserts engine.enrich_policies (L15295). GET round-trip returned exact match/match_field/enrich_fields stored. Persists in state.
Materialises .enrich-<name> from the policy source. Live: execute p1 -> {enrich_index:.enrich-p1, records:4008, status.phase:COMPLETE}.

Reindex bench(ids query)->audit-lifecycle-reindex-dest: {created:1,total:1,batches:1}. GET dest/_doc returns the real copied source; bench/_count still 4008. Source paginates search_after, index_document per hit (src 10869-10885).
PUT audit-lifecycle-ds -> {acknowledged:true}; GET shows backing index .ds-audit-lifecycle-ds-000001, gen 1. engine.create_data_stream creates backing index+alias+DataStream entry (engine.rs:744).

POST _rollover -> new_index .ds-audit-lifecycle-ds-000002,rolled_over:true; GET now lists 2 backing indices, generation=2. engine.rollover_data_stream bumps gen, creates index, updates alias (engine.rs:766).

PUT stores body in ilm_policies map; GET round-trips it (double-nested policy.policy from wrapping). Persists state but handler is pure KV insert (es_compat.rs:14225) — no rollover/delete phase enforcement runs.

PUT id=audit-scripts-tmpl -> {"acknowledged":true}; follow-up GET /_scripts/audit-scripts-tmpl returns found:true with the exact source stored. Src (15792): inserts into engine.search_templates map; state persists on re-GET.

MovingFunctions.max{1,5,3}->"5.0", unweightedAvg{2,4,6}->"4.0" (computed correctly); arbitrary painless 'doc[x].value*2'->null. Src (15828+): doc says 'sandboxed stub, NOT a code executor'; only whitelists MovingFunctions.max/min/sum/unweightedAvg + bare float parse.
200 _scroll_id=c3f8.., real bench hits (id 0001aa7b..), total.value 4008. Src 10500: stores ScrollContext{hits,position} in engine.scrolls, returns first page_size.
GET 200 new _scroll_id=579ce.., same real first-page doc 0001aa7b from bench. Same handler/code path as POST; genuine search+context store.
200; page1 ids 0025/0031, page2 ids 0056/005a (position advanced via ctx.position+=page_size, src 10666), total 4008. Reads engine.scrolls; 404 on unknown id.
{"num_freed":1,"succeeded":true}; next_scroll on that id then 404. Src 10738 removes id from engine.scrolls — real eviction.
200 {"id":"f424.."}; src 16297 inserts PitContext{indices,index_max_seq snapshot,expires_at} into engine.pits; close then frees exactly 1 (see DELETE /_pit).
First call num_freed:1 succeeded:true; repeat num_freed:0 succeeded:false. Src 16440 engine.pits.remove(id) — reflects real stored PIT state.
Real EQL: translates `<cat> where <cond>` (==,!=,>,>=,<,<=, and/or) to DSL + runs via Index::search. Live: 'any where model=="claude-haiku-4-5"' -> 3843 real events, took 32ms.
200 is_running:false; term tenant:acme returned hit 00773e35 with BM25 score 1.69 (real filtered search). Src 18003 runs idx.search then stores in engine.async_searches. Synchronous (no true async).
200 returns the stored result (same id 5e2398d3, embedded response.hits). Src 18050 engine.async_searches.get(id); 500 after delete — reflects real store.
{"acknowledged":true}; subsequent GET returns 500 'No async search found'. Src 18065 engine.async_searches.remove(id) — genuine removal (re-get 500 not 404, minor).
200 SELECT model,tenant FROM bench LIMIT 3 -> rows [[claude-haiku-4-5,northwind],...]. Src 18093 parse_sql -> idx.search, projects fields to columns/rows from real bench docs.
Real doc returned found:true with full _source from bench; missing id found:false. src 9625 idx.get_document(). Minor: _version/_seq_no hardcoded 1/0.
matched:true for match_all on real id, returns real _source; src 16901 runs Ids+query search to compute matched. Explanation tree value fixed at 1.0 (synthetic score) but match flag is real.
Same handler; match_all matched:true, term intent:summarize returns real doc; matched computed via idx.search Ids filter (src 16895).
GET bench/_count -> count:4008 (idx.stats doc_count, src 9537), matches index size.
term cache_hit:true -> count:1718 via size:0 search total (src 9527); computed, not canned.
_count -> 4018 across 19 indices (_shards.total:19); delegates to count_docs _all summing per-index stats (src 9434).
Same handler as GET; delegates _all to count_docs, real summed counts (src 9427).
match_all size:0 -> total 4021 across 21 shards (src 3804 delegates to search Path '*'). Hits/totals real. (Shares the agg-subset limitation noted on /:index/_search.)
Same handler delegating to search('*'); cross-index match_all totals real.

Hits/filtering real (term model -> total 1112, real _source). BUT stats agg latency_ms count:256 vs 4008 docs; terms intent doc-qa bucket 39 vs true 572. Aggs only cover ~256-doc subset (segments not aggregated).
Same handler; query/hits/total real but aggregations under-count to ~256 docs of 4008 (latency stats count:256). ES agg semantics broken on full index.
Parses the query via xerj_query::parse_request. Live: valid query -> {valid:true}; malformed query -> {valid:false}.
Parses the query via xerj_query::parse_request. Live: valid query -> {valid:true}; malformed query -> {valid:false}.
fields=cost_usd,model,cache_hit -> cost_usd:double, cache_hit:boolean, model:text with searchable/aggregatable; from idx.schema() (src 10945). Matches real mapping.
Same handler reads live schema per index (src 10923); types reflect real bench mapping.
2-item ndjson -> responses totals 572 (intent doc-qa) and 4008 (match_all); each runs real idx.search (src 11099). Per-item totals correct.
mustache {{it}}=doc-qa rendered+parsed+executed, returns real hit _source (src 15500 render_template then idx.search 15565).
ndjson template rendered+executed -> response total 4008 from real search (src 15703).
source with {{v}}=hello -> template_output {query:{term:{f:hello}}}; real mustache substitution (src 15748).

field intent prefix 'd' -> terms:[doc-qa], complete:true. src 15938 brute-force match_all (size 10000) over _source extracting unique values. Correct here; caveat: caps at 10k-doc scan, not a true term-dict enum.
fields=latency_ms -> long, indices:[bench,audit-lifecycle-reindex-dest] over all 19 indices; iterates every idx.schema() (src 16687).

Computes precision from real search: details.q1.hits list real retrieved bench ids with positions; rated id matched rating:1, others -1 (src 18222 idx.search + relevant_retrieved precision calc).

200 {"acknowledged":true}; re-GET returns the exact stored config {"audit-snapshot-repo":{"settings":{"location":...},"type":"fs"}}. Source es_compat.rs:14705 inserts body into engine.snapshot_repos DashMap; state persists.

200 {"accepted":true,"snapshot":{state:SUCCESS,uuid,index_files:[real seg/wal/fst names]}}; files actually on disk at /tmp/xerj-snapshots-audit/.../segments. Source:14767 calls engine.create_snapshot which flush()es + copy_dir_recursive + writes manifest.json.

200 {"snapshots":[{...real manifest with file list...}]}; missing snapshot -> 404 index_not_found 'snapshot [nope-snap] missing'. Source:14786 reads engine.snapshots map keyed repo/snap, 404 if repo or snap absent.

200 {"accepted":true}; after restore audit-snapshot-idx count=2 (reopened), bench still 4008. Source:14819 calls engine.restore_snapshot: reads manifest, removes+recopies dir, Index::open reopens. Genuine fs restore.

Handler calls engine.list_indices().await and iterates engine.aliases (es_compat.rs:11578-11613). Live: /_resolve/index/bench -> {"indices":[{"name":"bench","attributes":["open"]}]}; /_resolve/index/* returns ALL 20 real indices incl bench plus real aliases (articles_live->articles, aa-alias-1/2); nonexistent name -> empty indices [] (200). Reflects genuine engine state.

Iterates state.tasks.list() over the real TaskRegistry (state.rs:204) keyed {node}:{id} (es_compat.rs:14096-14110). Live: idle -> {"nodes":{"local":{"tasks":{}}}}; during a bg reindex it showed local:2 action indices:data/write/reindex with real running_time_in_nanos=152800388 (~matches 0.15s sleep) and real start_time_in_millis. Task disappears on completion (RAII TaskHandle Drop removes it, state.rs:157).

state.tasks.get(&task_id) lookup (es_compat.rs:16345). Live on in-flight local:4: {"completed":false,"task":{...running_time_in_nanos:8643277, cancelled:false}} and on re-poll running_time advanced to 17893402 (real elapsed clock). Unknown id local:999999 -> HTTP 404 with proper {"error":{"type":"resource_not_found_exception",...},"status":404}.

cancel_task calls state.tasks.cancel() which sets a real shared AtomicBool (state.rs:195-199); effect is observable: after POST /_tasks/local:4/_cancel the task's cancelled field flipped false->true on next GET. Response shape correct (node_failures, tasks{} with the entry; empty tasks{} for unknown id). BUT no long-running handler honors the flag: reindex (es_compat.rs:10832 registers _task but loop 10859-10912 never reads is_cancelled()), delete_by_query (12627) and update_by_query (12698) likewise never check it — so the operation runs to completion regardless. Cancellation is observable state only, not effective; hence PARTIAL not REAL.
PUT audit-templates-it -> {"acknowledged":true}; GET returns stored patterns/priority:50/settings/mappings. Source l10338 inserts into engine.templates map; GET reads same map. Persists.
PUT audit-templates-ct ack:true; GET back returns the exact stored template body. Source l14276 inserts raw body into engine.component_templates; GET reads it. State reflected.

PUT audit-templates-lt ack:true; GET shows index_patterns coerced str->array and settings flattened/stringified to {"index.number_of_shards":"3"} (ES storage form). Source l18876 inserts into legacy_templates. REAL.

POST .../audit-templates-it-foo -> matched:"audit-templates-it" with that template's real settings/mappings (genuine glob+priority match, l18996). But ignores component-template composition (composed_of) and always returns overlapping:[].
200 {"acknowledged":true}; re-GET returns exact config w/ injected id -> persists to engine.transforms dashmap. Src 18509: insert only, no validation; _start is 501 so aggregator never runs.
501 not_implemented_exception 'Transform job execution... _start does not actually run the aggregator'. Src 18575: hardcoded not_implemented_yet(), no engine call.

501 'Rollup job execution... _start does not run the rollup'. Src 18680: not_implemented_yet() stub.
501 '..._stop has nothing to stop'. Src 18691: not_implemented_yet() stub, no job state.

200 all counters 0, follow_stats.indices=[]. Src 18768: State(_state) unused, returns fixed JSON literal -> static, never reflects engine state.

Source (es_compat.rs:15534) inserts body into state.engine.watches keyed by id and returns created=true (201) vs 200 on overwrite. Live: PUT /_watcher/watch/audit-watch-1 -> 201 created=true; GET /_watcher/watch/audit-watch-1 -> 200 found=true and watch body echoes exactly what was stored ({"actions":{},"trigger":{"schedule":{"interval":"10s"}}}). Genuine persistence in the in-process watch store. (Canned result.condition.met=true is cosmetic; storage is real.)

Source (es_compat.rs:15597) does state.watcher_active.store(true). Live: after POST /_watcher/_start, GET /_xpack reports features.watcher.enabled=true. Toggle is genuinely reflected in cluster state (xpack_info reads the same AtomicBool at es_compat.rs:16189).

Source (es_compat.rs:15604) does state.watcher_active.store(false). Live: after POST /_watcher/_stop, GET /_xpack reports features.watcher.enabled=false; re-starting flips it back to true. Verified both directions against shared AppState.watcher_active.

Source (es_compat.rs:18764) calls ingest_monitoring_ndjson (es_compat.rs:19498) which get_or_create_index('xerj-monitoring') and idx.index_document() per NDJSON source line (meta/source alternation). Live: GET /xerj-monitoring/_count=2 before; POST 2-doc ndjson -> 200; after _refresh count=4 (+2). Real ingest into the engine. Caveat: response always returns errors:false/took:0 (ingest result is discarded via `let _ =`), so per-doc failures are not surfaced — but ingestion itself is genuine.

Source (es_compat.rs:16174-16227) reads state.license and state.watcher_active. Live: license block {uid:AUDIT-UID-12345,type:platinum,mode:platinum,status:active,expiry:4102444800000} exactly matches GET /_license after the PUT. features.watcher.enabled returned false after POST /_watcher/_stop and true after /_watcher/_start, proving it reflects AppState.watcher_active (not a constant). Other feature flags consistent with /_xpack/usage.
Real usage counts from engine stores: live watcher.count.total=1 after putting a watch; dense_vector_fields_count scanned from mappings; ilm/data_stream counts from their stores.

Source (es_compat.rs:16298-16319) returns the built-in single-node owner identity. Live response: username 'xerj', roles ['superuser'], full_name 'Xerj Administrator', enabled true, native realm, authentication_type 'realm' — exactly the upgrade target (xerj/superuser). Static is correct here since xerj has no multi-user store.

Source (es_compat.rs:16325-16358) generates a v4-UUID id, two-UUID secret, base64 api_key and base64(id:api_key) encoded credential. Two live calls returned distinct ids (8e887751… vs 5cc6cb78…) and distinct api_key/encoded values, proving genuine per-call uniqueness. Caveat (per source comment): keys are not stored, so not re-authenticatable — but there is no auth subsystem to persist into and the upgrade scope ('a real unique key') is met.

Source (es_compat.rs:16392-16397) clones state.license under RwLock. Live: initial GET showed default basic license; after PUT it returned type/mode 'platinum', uid 'AUDIT-UID-12345', expiry 4102444800000 — proving it reflects persisted in-process state, not a constant.

Source (es_compat.rs:16403-16439) unwraps {license:{}}/{licenses:[]}/bare object and merges fields into state.license under write lock. Live: PUT platinum license returned {acknowledged:true,license_status:valid}; subsequent GET /_license and GET /_xpack both reflected the merged platinum/AUDIT-UID values (untouched fields like issued_to/max_nodes preserved), confirming real merge+persist.
